parsed_review.summary: CP00-741 is a descriptor-only RP24 Korean Legal Depth bridge pack (risk C, 150 units, RP24.P05.M06.S21-RP24.P06.M03.S10) and its closeout claims are supported. Verified by direct Read/Grep: (1) the CP741 binding/requirements are internally consistent and match the baseline, manifest plan_binding_snapshot, README, and contract; deliverable distribution {impl:61,fixture:27,claude_review:7,security_audit:23,test:17,hermes_evidence:3,ui:12} sums to 150 and equals KOREAN_LEGAL_CP741_REQUIREMENTS.deliverable_counts, enforced by validators.js validateCoverage and asserted by model.test.js. (2) Descriptor-only/no-write/no-real-data boundaries hold: manifest production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, writes_product_state=false, runtime_opened=false, no_real_data=true; every case-set row sets real_client_data_included/legal_source_payload_included/external_credential_included/raw_payload_included=false and all HWPX/court/litigation/corporate/clause runtime flags=false, with validateDescriptor rejecting any violation. (3) Claude remains read-only and non-final: authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, claude_packet.read_only=true. (4) Hermes is control-plane only: evidence packets emit_runtime_receipt=false, writes_product_state=false, receipt_shape=descriptor_only_korean_legal_no_write. (5) Source grep found no fetch/exec/fs-write/network/credential/PII. (6) Contract projection latest_pack=CP00-741, cp722-740 historical, validation.valid=true with zero \"valid\": false. (7) Review-evidence hardening: auth preflight passed (exit 0, 1434-byte valid JSON), read-tools mode allowed, no forbidden tools, prompt within limit, no invalid review attempts. Provenance: the 10 validation commands plus npm test/build are recorded exit 0 in command-evidence.json (agent-reported); I did not re-run them (read-only, no Bash) but confirmed source/descriptor/test/contract/manifest by direct read. The pending verdicts in claude-review-result.json, construction-inspection.json, and adjudication.md are the expected pre-receipt state, not defects. The pack makes no production_ready/runtime/enterprise-trust claim, so per the authority-boundary guidance the CP closeout goal is met and neither pack nor goal closeout is blocked. One informational P3 about finalization hygiene. The dirty progress-control-room files are correctly classified as unrelated/preserved (UI non-CP track) and are out of scope for CP00-741.

findings.title: Closeout finalization fields are still pending and must be flipped only by deterministic tooling, not hand-edited
findings.details: At review time the closeout artifacts are in their expected pre-receipt state: claude-review-result.json has status=pending, exactly_one_valid_pack_level_claude_review_run=false, review_receipt_ref=null; construction-inspection.json has verdict=PENDING, pack_level_claude_review_completed=false, korean_legal_p05_p06_fixture_permission_bridge_descriptor_verified=false; adjudication.md is in_progress; command-evidence.json shows status=in_progress, gate_outcome=pending_pack_evidence_creation, hermes_gate.status=pending. These are by-design (they are finalized after a valid Claude review receipt is recorded) and are not defects, but they are the only gating items left before closeout. This finding exists to prevent the pack from being marked closed with stale/incorrect values or via manual edits.
findings.recommendation: Let the deterministic closeout pipeline record this PASS as the single valid pack-level review receipt and then flip review_receipt_ref, exactly_one_valid_pack_level_claude_review_run, pack_level_claude_review_completed, the descriptor_verified flag, the construction-inspection verdict, and adjudication status accordingly. Do not hand-edit these fields. Keep manifest production_ready/runtime_ready=false (descriptor-only). Confirm no second pack-level review run is recorded.
